(6) Learning Hexo from Scratch - Hexo Commands You Can't Forget
This post walks through the more important Hexo commands. We've actually touched a few of them already in earlier posts, so here I'll bring them all together in one place.
(5) Learning Hexo from Scratch - Your First Post
Next up we'll create your first blog post. Before we start, I'd recommend using Visual Studio Code to write your articles.
(4) Learning Hexo from Scratch - Understanding the Hexo Directory Structure
Now we'll get familiar with the Hexo directory structure. But heads up — in the previous post we only installed Hexo, we haven't actually created a Hexo project yet.
(3) Learning Hexo from Scratch - How to Install Hexo

This post covers installing Hexo on both Windows and Mac. After this one, the rest of the series will mainly use Mac for examples — the only real difference is in the install step, and everything past that is basically the same.
(2) Learning Hexo from Scratch - What Is Hexo?
Before we get into actually learning Hexo, let's spend a minute on what Hexo actually is and where it came from.

Why bother writing a whole series about Hexo when there are plenty of ready-made blog platforms? Honestly, Hexo is one of the easiest tools to start with — you just need to grok a few basics and you can spin up your own blog pretty fast. So no more rambling, let's start trying to build your first blog from zero.

Git Flow and commit conventions are usually a pain point for Git beginners, since you rarely run into team collaboration early on. So this post is a primer on what Git Flow is and why we bother with commit conventions.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (32) - Building a Restart Button From Scratch

A lighter one this time, picking up right where the last post left off. In this article we build a restart button for the Game Over screen — drawing the button with geometry, adding the label text, wiring up the click handler, and resetting everything back to its starting state.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (31): Showing Game Over and Start Text Messages

Time to build the text message that pops up when the player dies from a collision. The trick here isn't the text itself — it's juggling two Containers, one shown and one hidden. This post walks through the death screen, the start screen, and how to flip between them with PixiJS.
JavaScript Core Concepts (30): Shallow Copy vs Deep Copy for Objects

Objects in JavaScript are passed by reference, which means copying them is trickier than it looks. In this post we'll dig into shallow copy vs deep copy — how Object.assign and the spread syntax both fall short, why the JSON.stringify trick works but has nasty edge cases, and what to reach for when you need a real deep clone.
